theme: Laser cutter that warps its own sheet
title: Laser Cutter That Warps Its Own Sheet
seed_problem: valued rectangles nested on a sheet plus a cutting order under a self-heating scrap field
mutation: composite: the placement of parts and the order they are cut are fused into ONE ordered space-time layout; a reciprocal decay-in-time-and-space heat field (contour-order-coupling + thermal-decay-field) scraps any part whose accumulated field at cut-time exceeds a threshold, so irregular-nesting feasibility becomes a function of the schedule, not just the packing.
innovation_hook: The score couples a spatial packing to a temporal schedule through a decaying field, so the true decision variable is a space-time layout, not a layout plus a tour.
strategies: trivial: drop a few parts on a widely-spaced grid and cut them in input order (reproduces the checker baseline)

greedy (obvious): cram every part densely with a next-fit shelf pack to maximize placed value, then apply a farthest-first cooling cut order and cut everything -- max density is thermally unsalvageable by any schedule and doomed cuts still heat neighbours
strong (insight): co-design the space-time layout -- leave thermal gaps, schedule remaining adjacencies apart, and cut ONLY the parts that survive; iterate placement + order + subset together
an optimal solver packs adaptively tighter where the schedule can hide the heat and picks a higher-value surviving subset -> headroom above strong
